input.title: Subscription Box Service
input.description: Subscription Box Service is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Next.js and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, Stripe for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Plan Customer Registration Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for customer registration and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Implement complete customer registration functionality; All customer registration operations must be validated and authorized; System shall maintain audit log for customer registration actions.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for customer registration is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for customer registration are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for customer registration can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Customer Registration Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for customer registration on Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Stripe. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Implement complete customer registration functionality; All customer registration operations must be validated and authorized; System shall maintain audit log for customer registration actions.
acceptance_criteria: Core customer registration business rules are implemented for Subscription Box Service
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for customer registration
API/service behavior for customer registration is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Customer Registration Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for customer registration with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The customer registration user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for customer registration behave correctly
The customer registration UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Customer Registration Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for customer registration. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for customer registration supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for customer registration are defined correctly
Migrations for customer registration can be applied safely in target environments

FR-01.title: Harden Customer Registration Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to customer registration. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for customer registration are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in customer registration are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for customer registration passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write Customer Registration Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for customer registration. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for customer registration are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for customer registration are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical customer registration scenarios
